---
title: "# About This Book {.unnumbered}"
number-sections: false
---

This introductory textbook presents ICT as the service infrastructure through
which libraries, archives, records centers, museums, and other information
organizations store, describe, retrieve, deliver, preserve, protect, govern,
and improve information services.

The completed manuscript contains a detailed twelve-chapter course outline and
all twelve chapters, progressing from ICT infrastructure, networking, databases,
and metadata through digital services, retrieval, programming, APIs,
accessibility, preservation, cybersecurity, data literacy, and capstone
integration.

Unless otherwise indicated, this book is licensed under the **Creative Commons
Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (CC BY-NC-SA
4.0)**.
